UTM Formula Student Electric Vehicle — Design Project

As Drivetrain Department Lead, I managed a 5-member team through the complete design phase of UTM's first Formula Student Electric vehicle. Our department was responsible for the drivetrain system, cockpit ergonomics, and vehicle dynamics interfaces — delivering fully detailed SolidWorks assemblies across six subsystems.

The project reached full vehicle design completion in SolidWorks before being paused due to external constraints — meaning all engineering deliverables were met on schedule.

Full vehicle render — SolidWorks

Vehicle System Overview

Full Vehicle — Exploded View

The vehicle was designed as a multi-department collaboration. The exploded view shows the complete system integration across chassis, suspension, aerodynamics, powertrain, and cockpit departments. Subsystems highlighted below indicate direct team ownership.

Team Scope:
Driveshaft Steering Pedal Box Driver Seat Harness Mounts Firewall Underbody Chassis (separate dept.) Suspension (separate dept.) Aerodynamics (separate dept.)

Component Designs

Cockpit

Pedal Box Assembly

Dual-pedal brake and throttle assembly designed for FSAE driver accommodation requirements. The assembly integrates dual brake master cylinders for front-rear brake bias control, mounted on a structural base plate with lightening cutouts.

  • Dual master cylinder configuration for front/rear brake bias adjustment
  • Structural base plate with lightening cutouts to reduce mass
  • Designed to accommodate 95th percentile driver reach per FSAE cockpit rules
  • Pedal pivot geometry optimised for consistent pedal feel across stroke
  • Full SolidWorks assembly with hardware modelled (fasteners, clevis pins, rod ends)
Drivetrain / Steering

Steering Assembly

Rack-and-pinion steering system with upper and lower universal joints to accommodate the steering column angle relative to the rack. The custom steering wheel incorporates ergonomic cutouts for driver grip and weight reduction.

  • Rack-and-pinion mechanism for precise, low-friction steering response
  • Double U-joint column to accommodate column angle to rack geometry
  • Custom steering wheel designed for driver ergonomics and minimum mass
  • Column and rack positioned to clear chassis members and driver knee envelope
  • Assembly fully constrained in SolidWorks with mating conditions validated
Structural

Firewall & Structural Panels

The firewall — visible as the vertical panel integrated into the rear of the spaceframe — forms the mandatory separation between the high-voltage accumulator bay and the driver cockpit, as required by FSAE EV rules. Our team designed this panel and the underbody surface to interface correctly with the chassis geometry produced by the chassis department.

  • Firewall designed per FSAE EV rules requiring physical separation of HV accumulator and cockpit zones
  • Mounting interface coordinated with chassis team rollhoop and rear bulkhead geometry
  • Underbody panel designed for ground clearance compliance and aerodynamic underfloor flow
  • Panel geometry modelled to minimise weight while maintaining required rigidity
  • Cross-team integration: our mounting holes aligned to chassis tube positions defined by chassis department

Selected Academic Projects

CAD Design · Mechanism Concept

Traffic Cone Dropper & Retriever

Semi-automated vehicle-mounted mechanism concept to reduce manual roadside cone handling, improve worker safety, and support faster cone deployment/retrieval.

  • Developed the product scope, operating sequence, PDS, concept sketches, and final mechanism concept.
  • Considered manufacturability, portability, safety, durability, cost, and vehicle mounting requirements.
SolidWorks · Technical Drawing

Mini Heat Sealer CAD Assembly

Modelled the casing and internal layout of a mini heat sealer, including battery, PCB, buttons, LED, charging port, and exploded assembly drawing.

  • Produced part/assembly modelling and working drawing documentation.
MATLAB · Vibration Analysis

Industrial Machine Vibration Isolation

Analysed a 1DOF industrial machine support system and compared isolator layouts using natural frequency and damping ratio results.

  • Used MATLAB and hand calculations to compare five isolator placement concepts.
  • Selected the strongest setup based on vibration reduction and practical machine reliability.
Thermodynamics · MATLAB

Modified Brayton Cycle Gas Turbine Analysis

Modelled a modified Brayton cycle with intercooling, reheating, and regeneration for a 100 MW gas turbine power plant case study.

  • Created MATLAB scripts to calculate mass flow rate, thermal efficiency, work ratio, compressor work, turbine work, heat input, and exhaust temperature.
  • Generated sensitivity plots for thermal efficiency against inlet air temperature and compressor efficiency.

Relevant Laboratory Experience

Mechanical Systems & Testing

Rotor balancing, engine performance testing, vibration-related calculations, brake power, fuel consumption, thermal efficiency, and data-based discussion of experimental results.

Manufacturing & Materials

Metal cutting, SPC quality evaluation, Jominy end-quench hardenability testing, machining variables, control charts, hardness-distance curves, and measurement tools.
